perturbation.original_text: "Territory" means the countries and/or territories listed in Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: "Territory" means the countries and/or territories as determined solely by the Reseller and communicated in writing to the Supplier.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Territory' from being listed in Schedule 1 to being determined by the Reseller creates a contradiction. Section 2.1 initially restricts Reseller's activities to the territory defined in Schedule 1, but with the new definition, the Reseller has the ability to determine the Territory, giving them the power to change their territory without any control by the Supplier. This contradicts the exclusivity and limited rights granted to the reseller in the original text of Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment During the Term (defined below) and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement (including, but not limited to Section 12.4 hereof), Supplier hereby (i) appoints Reseller, and Reseller accepts the appointment, as a non‐ exclusive reseller of the Products in the Territory. In connection with the foregoing appointment, Supplier hereby grants Reseller a non-transferable, revocable, limited right to resell, market, promote, stimulate interest in, and solicit Orders by Customers and/or End Users in the Territory for the Products and to provide services in connection with those activities. Reseller shall be solely responsible for all of its costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Supplier reserves any and all rights with respect to the Products not expressly granted in this Agreement. Without limiting the right of the Reseller to receive any and all Paid-For Products (including but not limited to under Section 4.2 hereof), nothing in this Agreement shall entitle the Reseller to any priority of supply in relation to the Products as against any other distributor or customer of the Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Territory' grants the Reseller unilateral control over its scope, directly conflicting with the original definition that limited it to Schedule 1. This undermines the Supplier's control and the exclusivity provision in the Contradicted Text, creating a strong contradiction where the Reseller's rights and limitations are no longer clearly defined, impacting the enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller shall be solely responsible for all of its costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier shall be partially responsible for Reseller's costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products, with the specific amount to be decided at the end of each fiscal quarter, in Supplier's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying section 2.1 to state that 'Supplier shall be partially responsible for Reseller's costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products, with the specific amount to be decided at the end of each fiscal quarter, in Supplier's sole discretion' creates a contradiction with the original statement that 'Reseller shall be solely responsible for all of its costs and expenses related to the resale of the Products.' This uncertainty could lead to disputes about who bears the expenses of reselling the products. This change directly contradicts section 14.2 where it specifies that 'The relationship between the Parties is that of supplier and purchaser.' and 'Reseller is an independent contractor and is not the legal representative, agent, joint venture, partner, or employee of Supplier.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The relationship between the Parties is that of supplier and purchaser. Reseller is an independent contractor and is not the legal representative, agent, joint venture, partner, or employee of Supplier. Reseller may describe itself as Supplier's 'authorized distributor', but may not hold itself out as the Supplier's agent. Reseller has no authority to assume or create any obligations of any kind or to make any representations or warranties, whether express or implied, on behalf of the Supplier, nor to bind the Supplier in any respect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Supplier will be partially responsible for the Reseller's costs, which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating that the Reseller is an independent contractor and not an agent of the Supplier, having no authority to create any obligations on behalf of the Supplier. The original text reinforces the Reseller's sole responsibility for costs, whereas the changed text assigns partial responsibility to the Supplier, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller agrees to reimburse Supplier for the reasonable out of pocket costs and expenses incurred by Supplier in connection with the shipment and delivery of the Products, and of obtaining the foregoing registrations, licenses, permits, certificated and other documentation, provided that Supplier submits an invoice therefore in reasonable detail (and unless and solely to the extent such reimbursements is prohibited by Section 14.9 or prohibited under other applicable laws).
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller will never reimburse Supplier for any costs or expenses, unless Supplier expressly waives this condition in writing for each instance.
perturbation.explanation: This modification to section 3.2 creates a direct contradiction by reversing the original payment arrangement. The original text states the Reseller agrees to reimburse the Supplier for shipping and delivery costs, with stipulations on how the invoice must be handled and exceptions in Section 14.9. The modified text, however, asserts that 'Reseller will never reimburse Supplier for any costs or expenses, unless Supplier expressly waives this condition in writing for each instance', thereby making it Supplier's responsibility to reverse the condition. This creates a direct in-text contradiction with Schedule 1, specifically the 'Price' entry: '$*** per Product unit + $*** for shipment and tax (subject to any tax changes)' because the shipment and tax costs are not accounted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schedule 1 Territory, Products, Price, Ordering Address, Notices Territory USA Products Walabot HOME hardware device Price $*** per Product unit + $*** for shipment and tax (subject to any tax changes) MOQ *** units Address to which Orders should be sent Supplier's Ordering Email address: logistics@walabot.com Address for Notices to the Supplier Vayyar Ltd. 3 Avraham Giron St., POB. 325, Yehud 5621717, Israel Address for Notices to Reseller Inde Living Holdings, Inc 1462 Rudder Lane Knoxville, TN 37919 Vayyar Wire Transfer Details *** Payment Terms ***
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct and significant contradiction. The original text specifies that the Reseller agrees to reimburse the Supplier for shipping costs. The changed text explicitly states that 'Reseller will never reimburse Supplier for any costs or expenses'. This directly contradicts Schedule 1, which states the 'Price' as '$*** per Product unit + $*** for shipment and tax'. The changed text makes it unclear how shipment and tax costs will be covered, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about pricing terms.
